topic: blocking malolactic fermentation to preserve freshness

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A winemaker wants to preserve the crisp acid profile and primary fruit of a white wine.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Blocking malolactic fermentation prevents lactic acid bacteria from converting sharper malic acid into softer lactic acid.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: More malic acidity and freshness remain, producing a crisper, more linear style without MLF-derived creamy character.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: More malic acidity and freshness remain, producing a crisper, more linear style without MLF-derived creamy character.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
